records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.377 - The Imam (as) said:
"Whoever dies in one of the two sanctuaries (Makkah or Madinah) will be safe from the Great Terror on the Day of Resurrection."
records.arabicText: 377 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ مَاتَ فِي أَحَدِ اَلْحَرَمَيْنِ أَمِنَ مِنَ اَلْفَزَعِ اَلْأَكْبَرِ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.377 - The Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever dies in one of the two sanctuaries (Makkah or Madinah) will be safe from the Great Terror on the Day of Resurrection."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.378 - The Imam (as) said:
"If a woman dies during childbirth (in her postpartum state), no record of her deeds will be unfurled on the Day of Resurrection."
records.arabicText: 378 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِذَا مَاتَتْ فِي نِفَاسِهَا لَمْ يَنْشُرْ لَهَا دِيوَانٌ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.378 - The Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a woman dies during childbirth (in her postpartum state), no record of her deeds will be unfurled on the Day of Resurrection."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.379 - The Imam (as) said:
"The death of a stranger (someone who dies away from their home) is martyrdom."
records.arabicText: 379 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَوْتُ اَلْغَرِيبِ شَهَادَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.379 - The Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The death of a stranger (someone who dies away from their home) is martyrdom."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.380 - The Imam (as) said regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious:
"No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in which land it will die" (Surah Luqman 31:34).
Imam (as) said; "From one step to another."
records.arabicText:
380 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «۝ وَ مٰا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ مٰا ذٰا تَكْسِبُ غَداً وَ مٰا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ بِأَيِّ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ ۝»
فَقَالَ «مِنْ قَدَمٍ إِلَى قَدَمٍ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: "From one step to another."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.381 - The Imam (as) said:
"When a believer dies, the places on earth where they used to worship Allah (swt), the door through which their deeds would ascend, and their place of prostration weep for them."
records.arabicText: 381 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا مَاتَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ بَكَتْ عَلَيْهِ بِقَاعُ اَلْأَرْضِ اَلَّتِي كَانَ يَعْبُدُ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهَا وَ اَلْبَابُ اَلَّذِي كَانَ يَصْعَدُ مِنْهُ عَمَلُهُ وَ مَوْضِعُ سُجُودِهِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.382 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Whoever considers tomorrow as part of their lifespan has not kept good company with death."
records.arabicText: 382 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ عَدَّ غَداً مِنْ أَجَلِهِ فَقَدْ أَسَاءَ صُحْبَةَ اَلْمَوْتِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.382 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever considers tomorrow as part of their lifespan has not kept good company with death."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.383 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon Khadijah {s.a} while she was experiencing what she {s.a} was enduring (her final illness).
He (sw) said to her: "In spite of what we see happening to you, Khadijah {s.a}, when you meet your co-wives in the Hereafter, give them my greetings of peace."
She {s.a} asked: "Who are they, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)?"
He (sw) replied: "Mary {s.a}, the daughter of Imran (as), and Kulthum {s.a}, the sister of Musa (as), and Asiya {s.a}, the wife of Pharaoh."
She {s.a} responded: "With pleasure, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
records.arabicText: 383 - وَ دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَلَى خَدِيجَةَ وَ هِيَ لِمَا بِهَا فَقَالَ لَهُ «بِالرَّغْمِ مِنَّا مَا نَرَى بِكِ يَا خَدِيجَةُ فَإِذَا قَدِمْتِ عَلَى ضَرَائِرِكِ فَأَقْرِئِيهِنَّ اَلسَّلاَمَ» فَقَالَتْ مَنْ هُنَّ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ قَالَ « مَرْيَمُ اِبْنَةُ عِمْرَانَ وَ كُلْثُمُ أُخْتُ مُوسَى وَ آسِيَةُ اِمْرَأَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ » قَالَتْ بِالرِّفَاءِ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.arabicText: 384 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «ضَمِنْتُ لِسِتَّةٍ اَلْجَنَّةَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ بِصَدَقَةٍ فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ وَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ يَعُودُ مَرِيضاً فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ وَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ مُجَاهِداً فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ وَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ حَاجّاً فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ وَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ إِلَى اَلْجُمُعَةِ فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ ' وَ رَجُلٌ خَرَجَ فِي جِنَازَةِ رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ فَمَاتَ فَلَهُ اَلْجَنَّةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.384 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.385 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"The honor of the deceased is their hastening (to be buried)."
records.arabicText: 385 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «كَرَامَةُ اَلْمَيِّتِ تَعْجِيلُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.385 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The honor of the deceased is their hastening (to be buried)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.386 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "I do not want to find among you a man whose deceased loved one died at night and he waits until morning, or a man whose deceased loved one died during the day and he waits until night. Do not wait for the rising or setting of the sun with your dead. Hurry them to their resting places; may Allah (swt) have mercy on you."
The people replied: "And may Allah (swt) have mercy on you, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
records.arabicText: 386 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «لاَ أُلْفِيَنَّ مِنْكُمْ رَجُلاً مَاتَ لَهُ مَيِّتٌ لَيْلاً فَانْتَظَرَ بِهِ اَلصُّبْحَ وَ لاَ رَجُلاً مَاتَ لَهُ مَيِّتٌ نَهَاراً فَانْتَظَرَ بِهِ اَللَّيْلَ لاَ تَنْتَظِرُوا بِمَوْتَاكُمْ طُلُوعَ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ لاَ غُرُوبَهَا عَجِّلُوا بِهِمْ إِلَى مَضَاجِعِهِمْ يَرْحَمُكُمُ اَللَّهُ» فَقَالَ اَلنَّاسُ وَ أَنْتَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ يَرْحَمُكَ اَللَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.386 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I do not want to find among you a man whose deceased loved one died at night and he waits until morning, or a man whose deceased loved one died during the day and he waits until night. Do not wait for the rising or setting of the sun with your dead. Hurry them to their resting places; may Allah (swt) have mercy on you."
The people replied: "And may Allah (swt) have mercy on you, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.387 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "In what Musa ibn Imran (as) privately spoke to his Lord (azj), the Mighty and Glorious,
Musa (as) said: 'O’ Lord (azj), what is the reward for visiting the sick?'
Allah (swt) replied: 'I will appoint an angel to visit them in their grave until the Day of Resurrection.'
Musa (as) asked: 'O’ Lord (azj), what about the one who washes the deceased?'
Allah (swt) replied: 'I will cleanse them of their sins as on the day their mother gave birth to them.'"
records.arabicText: 387 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَانَ فِيمَا نَاجَى بِهِ، مُوسَى بْنُ عِمْرَانَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنْ قَالَ يَا رَبِّ مَا بَلَغَ مِنْ عِيَادَةِ اَلْمَرِيضِ مِنَ اَلْأَجْرِ قَالَ أُوَكِّلُ بِهِ مَلَكاً يَعُودُهُ فِي قَبْرِهِ إِلَى مَحْشَرِهِ قَالَ يَا رَبِّ فَمَا لِمَنْ غَسَلَ اَلْمَوْتَى قَالَ أَغْسِلُهُ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ كَيَوْمَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.387 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"In what Musa ibn Imran (as) privately spoke to his Lord (azj), the Mighty and Glorious,
Musa (as) said: 'O’ Lord (azj), what is the reward for visiting the sick?'
Allah (swt) replied: 'I will appoint an angel to visit them in their grave until the Day of Resurrection.'
Musa (as) asked: 'O’ Lord (azj), what about the one who washes the deceased?'
Allah (swt) replied: 'I will cleanse them of their sins as on the day their mother gave birth to them.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.388 - The Imam (as) said:
"Whoever washes a deceased believer and fulfils the trust in that act, Allah (swt) will forgive them." It was asked: "How does one fulfil the trust in this matter?"
Imam (as) replied: "By not disclosing what they see and maintaining their duty until the deceased is buried."
records.arabicText: 388 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ غَسَّلَ مَيِّتاً مُؤْمِناً فَأَدَّى فِيهِ اَلْأَمَانَةَ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ» قِيلَ وَ كَيْفَ يُؤَدِّي فِيهِ اَلْأَمَانَةَ قَالَ «لاَ يُخْبِرُ بِمَا يَرَاهُ وَ حَدُّهُ إِلَى أَنْ يُدْفَنَ اَلْمَيِّتُ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.389 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Any believer who washes the body of another believer and says while turning it:
Allāhumma hādhā badan ‘abdika al-mu’min wa qad akhrajta rūḥahu minhu wa farraqta baynahumā fa‘afwuka ‘afwuka ‘afwuka
'O’ Allah (swt), this is the body of Your believing servant; You have taken their soul from it and separated them from each other, so forgive, forgive, forgive,'
Allah (swt) will forgive the washer's sins for a year, except for major sins."
records.arabicText: 389 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ غَسَّلَ مُؤْمِناً فَقَالَ إِذَا قَلَّبَهُ: اَللَّهُمَّ هَذَا بَدَنُ عَبْدِكَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ وَ قَدْ أَخْرَجْتَ رُوحَهُ مِنْهُ وَ فَرَّقْتَ بَيْنَهُمَا فَعَفْوَكَ عَفْوَكَ عَفْوَكَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ ذُنُوبَ سَنَةٍ إِلاَّ اَلْكَبَائِرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.389 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Any believer who washes the body of another believer and says while turning it:
Allāhumma hādhā badan ‘abdika al-mu’min wa qad akhrajta rūḥahu minhu wa farraqta baynahumā fa‘afwuka ‘afwuka ‘afwuka
'O’ Allah (swt), this is the body of Your believing servant; You have taken their soul from it and separated them from each other, so forgive, forgive, forgive,'
Allah (swt) will forgive the washer's sins for a year, except for major sins."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.390 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There is no believing servant who washes a deceased believer and says while washing them:
Rabbi ‘afwuka ‘afwuka
'O’ Lord (azj), Your forgiveness, Your forgiveness,' except that Allah (swt) forgives them."
records.arabicText: 390 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ مُؤْمِنٍ يَغْسِلُ مَيِّتاً مُؤْمِناً وَ يَقُولُ وَ هُوَ يَغْسِلُهُ رَبِّ عَفْوَكَ عَفْوَكَ إِلاَّ عَفَا اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.390 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There is no believing servant who washes a deceased believer and says while washing them:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Rabbi ‘afwuka ‘afwuka
'O’ Lord (azj), Your forgiveness, Your forgiveness,' except that Allah (swt) forgives them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.391 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"The one who washes the deceased should be the closest person to them or the one whom the guardian (Wali) of the deceased appoints for this task."
records.arabicText: 391 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «يَغْسِلُ اَلْمَيِّتَ أَوْلَى اَلنَّاسِ بِهِ أَوْ مَنْ يَأْمُرُهُ اَلْوَلِيُّ بِذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.391 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The one who washes the deceased should be the closest person to them or the one whom the guardian (Wali) of the deceased appoints for this task."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.392 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever washes a deceased person and conceals and keeps secret what they see, they will emerge from their sins like the day their mother gave birth to them."
records.arabicText: 392 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ غَسَّلَ مَيِّتاً فَسَتَرَ وَ كَتَمَ خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ كَيَوْمَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.392 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever washes a deceased person and conceals and keeps secret what they see, they will emerge from their sins like the day their mother gave birth to them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.393 - Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Saffar wrote to Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as) asking:
"What is the prescribed amount of water for washing the deceased, as it has been narrated that the junub (one in a state of major ritual impurity) bathes with six ratls of water and the menstruating woman with nine ratls of water ? Is there a prescribed amount of water for washing the deceased?"
And Imam (as) replied in his own handwriting, saying: "The prescribed washing of the deceased is to continue until they are purified, if Allah (swt), the Exalted, wills."
records.arabicText: 393 - وَ كَتَبَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلصَّفَّارُ إِلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: كَمْ حَدُّ اَلْمَاءِ اَلَّذِي يُغْسَلُ بِهِ اَلْمَيِّتُ كَمَا رَوَوْا أَنَّ اَلْجُنُبَ يَغْتَسِلُ بِسِتَّةِ أَرْطَالٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ وَ اَلْحَائِضَ بِتِسْعَةِ أَرْطَالٍ فَهَلْ لِلْمَيِّتِ حَدٌّ مِنَ اَلْمَاءِ اَلَّذِي يُغْسَلْ بِهِ فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «حَدُّ غُسْلِ اَلْمَيِّتِ يُغْسَلُ حَتَّى يَطْهُرَ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.393 - Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Saffar wrote to Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as) asking:
"What is the prescribed amount of water for washing the deceased, as it has been narrated that the junub (one in a state of major ritual impurity) bathes with six ratls of water and the menstruating woman with nine ratls of water ? Is there a prescribed amount of water for washing the deceased?"
And Imam (as) replied in his own handwriting, saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prescribed washing of the deceased is to continue until they are purified, if Allah (swt), the Exalted, wills."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.394 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Water should not be heated for washing the deceased."
records.arabicText: 394 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ يُسْخَنُ اَلْمَاءُ لِلْمَيِّتِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.394 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Water should not be heated for washing the deceased."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.395 - It is also narrated in another hadith: "Except if it is a cold winter, in which case, you should protect the deceased as you would protect yourself."
records.arabicText: 395 - وَ رُوِيَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ: «إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ شِتَاءً بَارِداً فَتُوَقِّيَ اَلْمَيِّتَ مِمَّا تُوَقِّي مِنْهُ نَفْسَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.395 - It is also narrated in another hadith:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Except if it is a cold winter, in which case, you should protect the deceased as you would protect yourself."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.397 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about washing the deceased in an open space.
Imam (as) replied: "There is no issue with it, but if they are covered with a curtain (for privacy), that is more beloved to me."
records.arabicText: 397 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَيِّتِ يُغْسَلُ فِي اَلْفَضَاءِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ وَ إِنْ سُتِرَ بِسِتْرٍ فَهُوَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.397 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about washing the deceased in an open space.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "There is no issue with it, but if they are covered with a curtain (for privacy), that is more beloved to me."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.398 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about whether it is permissible for a man to look at his wife when She dies or to wash her if there is no one else to wash her, and likewise, if a woman can do the same for her husband when he dies. Imam (as) replied:
"There is no issue with that. It is usually the woman's family who may dislike her husband seeing something he would dislike about her."
records.arabicText: 398 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ أَ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْظُرَ إِلَى اِمْرَأَتِهِ حِينَ تَمُوتُ أَوْ يَغْسِلُهَا إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ عِنْدَهَا مَنْ يَغْسِلُهَا وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ هَلْ تَنْظُرُ إِلَى مِثْلِ ذَلِكَ مِنْ زَوْجِهَا حِينَ يَمُوتُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ إِنَّمَا يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ أَهْلُ اَلْمَرْأَةِ كَرَاهِيَةَ أَنْ يَنْظُرَ زَوْجُهَا إِلَى شَيْءٍ يَكْرَهُونَهُ مِنْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.398 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about whether it is permissible for a man to look at his wife when She dies or to wash her if there is no one else to wash her, and likewise, if a woman can do the same for her husband when he dies. Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no issue with that. It is usually the woman's family who may dislike her husband seeing something he would dislike about her."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.399 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about about who performed the washing (Ghusl) of (the body of) Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A}?"
Imam (as) said: "The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) performed her washing because She was a ‘Al-Siddiqah’ (the most truthful and righteous one) ; and her ghusl is not permitted except by a ‘Al-Siddiq’ (truthful and righteous person)."
records.arabicText: 399 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ فَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ مَنْ غَسَلَهَا فَقَالَ «غَسَلَهَا أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِأَنَّهَا كَانَتْ صِدِّيقَةً لَمْ يَكُنْ لِيَغْسِلَهَا إِلاَّ صِدِّيقٌ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
And whoever touches a piece of the body of a dead human devoured by animals; if he has touched a bone, he must perform ghusl (ritual purification), but if there is no bone present, there is no obligation for ghusl.
And whoever touches a dead creature must wash his hands, but he is not required to perform the wash of touching; this obligation applies only to humans.
If someone touches a dead person before they have cooled (while their body is still warm), they are not required to perform ghusl. However, if they touch them after the body has cooled, they must perform ghusl. If the person is touched after the deceased has been washed (ritually purified), there is no ghusl required.
records.arabicText: ومن مس قطعة من جسد أكيل السبع فعليه الغسل إن كان فيما مس عظم وما لم يكن فيه عظم فلا غسل عليه في مسه، ومن مس ميتة فعليه أن يغسل يديه وليس عليه الغسل إنما يجب ذلك في الانسان وحده، ومن مس ميتا قبل الغسل بحرارته فلا غسل عليه، وإن مسه بعدما يبرد فعليه الغسل، ومن مسه بعدما يغسل فليس عليه غسل.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.400 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Touching a deceased person at the time of their death and after their washing, and kissing them, is not an issue”.
records.arabicText: 400 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَسُّ اَلْمَيِّتِ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ وَ بَعْدَ غُسْلِهِ وَ اَلْقُبْلَةُ لَيْسَ بِهَا بَأْسٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.400 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Touching a deceased person at the time of their death and after their washing, and kissing them, is not an issue”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.401 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the reason for placing the palm branch (jareedah),
Imam (as) replied: "Indeed, the punishment is averted from them (the deceased) as long as it remains fresh."
records.arabicText: 401 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ عِلَّةِ اَلْجَرِيدَةِ فَقَالَ «إِنَّهُ يَتَجَافَى عَنْهُ اَلْعَذَابُ مَا دَامَتْ رَطْبَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.401 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the reason for placing the palm branch (jareedah),
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, the punishment is averted from them (the deceased) as long as it remains fresh."
